title: racosperma oncinophyllum subsp. patulifolium
intro: Racosperma oncinophyllum subsp. patulifolium, also known as the Pat's Racosperma, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family. It is a small to medium-sized shrub native to Western Australia, known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant's leaves and stems are used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits like antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infused in water to create a herbal remedy, often used in Australian bush medicine.
